title: [v5.4-rhel] CVE-2025-22869: bump to golang.org/x/crypto@v0.36.0
body:
vendor: bump to golang.org/x/crypto@v0.36.0

Fixes: CVE-2025-22869
Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-82775
Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-82776
Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-81319
Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/OCPBUGS-52552

comments.body: Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
Cockpit tests failed for commit 946ccfa0ab6d706b81f1883ec2507034d9aed892. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

cockpit-podman's `testLifecycle{System,User}` fails a lot here, it cannot figure out the percentage of memory usage. The absolute value (MemUsage == 300MB) and limit are both ok, so how is round(MemUsage/memTotal) == 0? (`memTotal` comes from `/proc/meminfo`)

That test [is not generally flaky](https://ci-weather-cockpit.apps.ocp.cloud.ci.centos.org/tests.html?repo=cockpit-project%2Fcockpit-podman&days=5), and it does not happen on podman's main branch. The 5.4 branch seems to be special in some way, the same failure also happened in #25535 and #25496 (already merged).

I tried to locally reproduce the failure with the [PR's COPR build](https://dashboard.packit.dev/jobs/copr/2297853), but it always passes. I'll investigate this further in https://github.com/cockpit-project/cockpit-podman/pull/2045 -- for this PR you can ignore.

@Luap99 any idea why most tests here didn't run after the most recent push?

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe*, *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v5.4-rhel/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> @Luap99 any idea why most tests here didn't run after the most recent push?

Note these are rhel specific branches and only consumed in RHEL, we make the decision to stop running actual tests on them as it was not maintainable over the long run. As such I removed most of the CI things on these branches. (It doesn't make sense to test fedora jobs when the code is never shipped there)

Eventually the goal is to remove the branches completely from this repo and move them somewhere ekse where we can setup proper RHEL CI.

The actual v5.4 branch is used on fedora so if it is failing there as well it is certainly worth to look into why.
